Now Ricky, as his close associates are known to call him, is a simple man; an hvac salesman by day, practitioner of the Leo DiCaprio method of dating by night. One day we see him struggling to find a bathroom at Alfa Romero HVAC. He stumbles upon a single door. The letters on the door say “Peru.” “Huh,” he say as the door swings open. He walks in and finds himself in a hallway. “Where the fuck is the shitter?” The mauve colors and flickering light give him a sense of foreboding, but he continues on. As he gets deeper and deeper into the hallway he starts to see things out of the corner of his eye. A low din begins to make itself known in the outskirts of his auditory range. He is beginning to think this isn’t the shitter after all. He carries on. Flashes of light on the walls. Terrible, terrible smells. He can hear the din growing louder and louder. As he carries on the mauve begins to shift. Yellow, red, blue, all the colors of the rainbow swirl before him as the triumphant cacophony swells to a maximum. Ricky passes out. When he awakes he finds himself outside. Broad Street. His ears are ringing and his eyes are still getting used to the lights and colors. He faintly hears the words “it’s fun to stay at the…” he seems confused, as anyone would be. He rubs his eyes and starts to make out figures dancing and twirling about him, none of them old enough to remember 9/11. The songs and sounds grow more and more familiar to him. The song is more prevalent now. “ITS FUN TO STAY AT THE YMCA!!” People are celebrating. Ricky finds himself celebrating. As the camera pans back and the music swells, we find ourselves once again looking at a door. Only this time the letters Peru morph themselves into the much more flamboyant “Happy Pride!””

When he awakes he finds himself outside. Broad Street. His ears are ringing and his eyes are still getting used to the lights and colors. He faintly hears the words “it’s fun to stay at the…” he seems confused, as anyone would be. He rubs his eyes and starts to make out figures dancing and twirling about him, none of them old enough to remember 9/11. The songs and sounds grow more and more familiar to him. Dank: Loved “The lights flicker. The walls and floors are mauve. Welcome… to the Backrooms. Our story begins with a simple man, Richard Weiner. Now Ricky, as his close associates are known to call him, is a simple man; an hvac salesman by day, practitioner of the Leo DiCaprio method of dating by night. One day we see him struggling to find a bathroom at Alfa Romero HVAC. He stumbles upon a single door. The letters on the door say “Peru.” “Huh,” he say as the door swings open. He walks in and finds himself in a hallway. “Where the fuck is the shitter?” The mauve colors and flickering light give him a sense of foreboding, but he continues on. As he gets deeper and deeper into the hallway he starts to see things out of the corner of his eye. A low din begins to make itself known in the outskirts of his auditory range. He is beginning to think this isn’t the shitter after all. He carries on. Flashes of light on the walls. Terrible, terrible smells. He can hear the din growing louder and louder. As he carries on the mauve begins to shift. Yellow, red, blue, all the colors of the rainbow swirl before him as the triumphant cacophony swells to a maximum. Ricky passes out. When he awakes he finds himself outside. Broad Street. His ears are ringing and his eyes are still getting used to the lights and colors. He faintly hears the words “it’s fun to stay at the…” he seems confused, as anyone would be. He rubs his eyes and starts to make out figures dancing and twirling about him, none of them old enough to remember 9/11. The songs and sounds grow more and more familiar to him. The song is more prevalent now. “ITS FUN TO STAY AT THE YMCA!!” People are celebrating. Ricky finds himself celebrating. As the camera pans back and the music swells, we find ourselves once again looking at a door. Only this time the letters Peru morph themselves into the much more flamboyant “Happy Pride!””

Dank: Loved “The lights flicker. The walls and floors are mauve. Welcome… to the Backrooms. Our story begins with a simple man, Richard Weiner. Now Ricky, as his close associates are known to call him, is a simple man; an hvac salesman by day, practitioner of the Leo DiCaprio method of dating by night. One day we see him struggling to find a bathroom at Alfa Romero HVAC. He stumbles upon a single door. The letters on the door say “Peru.” “Huh,” he say as the door swings open. He walks in and finds himself in a hallway. “Where the fuck is the shitter?” The mauve colors and flickering light give him a sense of foreboding, but he continues on. As he gets deeper and deeper into the hallway he starts to see things out of the corner of his eye. A low din begins to make itself known in the outskirts of his auditory range. He is beginning to think this isn’t the shitter after all. He carries on. Flashes of light on the walls. Terrible, terrible smells. He can hear the din growing louder and louder. As he carries on the mauve begins to shift. Yellow, red, blue, all the colors of the rainbow swirl before him as the triumphant cacophony swells to a maximum. Ricky passes out. When he awakes he finds himself outside. Broad Street. His ears are ringing and his eyes are still getting used to the lights and colors. He faintly hears the words “it’s fun to stay at the…” he seems confused, as anyone would be. He rubs his eyes and starts to make out figures dancing and twirling about him, none of them old enough to remember 9/11. The songs and sounds grow more and more familiar to him. The song is more prevalent now. “ITS FUN TO STAY AT THE YMCA!!” People are celebrating. Ricky finds himself celebrating. As the camera pans back and the music swells, we find ourselves once again looking at a door. Only this time the letters Peru morph themselves into the much more flamboyant “Happy Pride!””
